source_evidence.title: DevHub account and sign-in guide
source_evidence.span: DevHub online permitting services require a wetten.overheid.nl sign-in flow.

source_evidence.title: Submit Plans Online / Single PDF Process
source_evidence.span: Drawing plans are prepared as one PDF while applications, calculations, and other supporting documents are separate PDFs.

source_evidence.title: File naming standards and preparing PDFs
source_evidence.span: PP&D guidance provides file naming and PDF preparation standards for uploaded plan review documents.

source_evidence.title: How to pay fees
source_evidence.span: Fee payment includes payment detail entry and a final submit-payment action.

source_evidence.title: DevHub permit application guide
source_evidence.span: Permit applications can include dynamic questions, save-for-later behavior, required uploads, acknowledgements, and post-submission status tracking.

source_evidence.title: Apply for permits
source_evidence.span: Some permit types must be submitted by email or other paths, so DevHub cannot be assumed to cover every permit.

source_evidence.title: Online permitting tools overview
source_evidence.span: Contractor license data on the DevHub profile can affect available fixture options for standard trade permits.

source_evidence.title: DevHub FAQs
source_evidence.span: Uploading corrections is an authenticated workflow with permit lookup and review-group status checks.

candidates.summary: Record whether the user has a DevHub-capable wetten.overheid.nl account before online permitting assistance.
candidates.subject: applicant or authorized representative
conditions: online permitting service is being used

candidates.summary: For plan review workflows, propose a required complete drawing plan set PDF distinct from applications, calculations, and other supporting PDFs.
candidates.subject: applicant

candidates.summary: Uploaded plan review documents should be checked against PP&D file naming and PDF preparation standards before staging.
candidates.subject: agent
candidates.object: upload file names and PDF preparation

candidates.summary: Fee payment workflows require a financial gate before payment detail entry or final submit-payment actions.
candidates.subject: agent
candidates.object: payment detail entry and final payment submission
conditions: fees are due or a payment workflow is opened

candidates.summary: Saved draft and post-submission status states should be reviewed for visible deadline or expiration messages before the agent proposes a next action.
candidates.subject: agent
candidates.object: visible deadline, expiration, or status messages
conditions: save-for-later or post-submission status tracking is used
candidates.deadline_or_temporal_scope: before proposing the next DevHub action

candidates.summary: Do not assume DevHub is the supported submission path for every permit type; route email or other official paths to manual review.
candidates.subject: agent
candidates.object: DevHub availability for every permit type
conditions: permit type is not explicitly covered by cited DevHub guidance

candidates.summary: For standard trade permits, available fixture options may depend on contractor license data in the DevHub profile.
candidates.subject: standard trade permit applicant
candidates.object: contractor license profile facts before fixture selection
conditions: standard trade permit includes fixture selection

candidates.summary: Correction uploads are consequential authenticated actions and require attendance, exact confirmation, and status review before upload.
candidates.subject: agent
